description: 5G connection quality monitoring — latency, jitter, packet loss, and uptime

panels.title: Current Status
panels.description: Live connection status based on the most recent probe

panels.title: Deployed Version
panels.description: Currently deployed version (short-hash). Backed by the towerwatch_build_info Prom gauge, re-emitted every tick — always shows the running version regardless of lookback window.

panels.title: Packet Loss
panels.description: Percentage of TCP probes that failed per 30-second cycle

panels.title: DNS Resolution Time
panels.description: DNS query time per nameserver (bypasses local cache)

panels.title: TCP Connection Time
panels.description: TCP handshake time to 8.8.8.8:443

panels.title: HTTP Latency Probe
panels.description: Timed 10KB download from Cloudflare CDN (every 5 min)

panels.title: Avg Download Speed
panels.description: Mean of all Ookla speedtest download samples in the selected time range. Sparse by design — runs are manual.

panels.title: Avg Upload Speed
panels.description: Mean of all Ookla speedtest upload samples in the selected time range. Sparse by design — runs are manual.

panels.title: M6 Signal Quality
panels.description: Nighthawk M6 signal metrics: RSRP, RSRQ, SINR

panels.title: Gateway Health
panels.description: TCP connect and HTTP response time to the local gateway. Works on any router — always populated regardless of vendor.

panels.title: Service Restarts
panels.description: All service restarts (planned deploys and unplanned crashes) in the selected time range.

panels.title: Avg HTTP Download
panels.description: Mean download throughput from passive HTTP samples (6×/day, random schedule). Gauge over the dashboard time range. Sample size is per-site (HTTP_THROUGHPUT_BYTES_OVERRIDE in credentials.py).

panels.title: Avg HTTP Upload
panels.description: Mean upload throughput from passive HTTP samples (6×/day, random schedule, paired with the download probe). Cellular uplinks are typically capacity-capped — expect a flat ceiling.

panels.title: Manual Speedtest History
panels.description: Ookla speedtest runs triggered via `towerwatch-speedtest`. Sparse by design — each run uses ~400 MB. Legend shows who triggered each run.

panels.title: Daily Avg Download Throughput (30d)
panels.description: One bar per calendar day, 30-day fixed window. Each bar is the mean of all towerwatch_http_throughput_mbps samples for that day. Time range is panel-fixed — does not follow the dashboard time picker.

panels.title: Daily Avg Upload Throughput (30d)
panels.description: One bar per calendar day, 30-day fixed window. Each bar is the mean of all towerwatch_http_upload_mbps samples for that day. Will appear empty until upload probe samples have accumulated.

panels.title: Latency (1h avg)
panels.description: Average ping RTT to 8.8.8.8 over the last hour. The Latency Golden Signal — high values mean the link is slow regardless of throughput.

panels.title: Uptime (range %)
panels.description: Percentage of time the link reported connected=1 over the dashboard time range. The Traffic Golden Signal. Replaces the broken Connection Uptime panel — uses avg_over_time directly so sub-5-min outages aren't masked.

panels.title: Packet Loss (1h max)
panels.description: Highest packet-loss percentage to Google or Cloudflare over the last hour. Excludes gateway loss (often legitimately high on cellular). The Errors Golden Signal.

panels.title: Saturation (6h)
panels.description: 6-hour average HTTP throughput as a percentage of the plan capacity ($max_download_mbps). 30–80% is healthy active use; >95% means the link is saturated; near 0% means light usage. The Saturation Golden Signal.

title: Towerwatch — 5G Connection Quality
